--- SOURCE 1: Federal Funding and Financing for Hydrogen Energy Production ... ---
URL: https://www.nga.org/publications/federal-funding-and-financing-for-hydrogen-energy-production-and-use/

SUMMARY:
This brief discusses the $7 billion funding announcement for the Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs program administered by the U.S. Department of Energy, the growing nationwide interest in hydrogen energy generation, and alternative federal funding and financing mechanisms for hydrogen generation projects. The H2Hubs program was created to fund between six to ten regional clean hydrogen hubs across the United States. In addition to the H2Hubs program, there are many federal programs and financing mechanisms that can be utilized by states and territories for hydrogen energy projects. The NGA Center for Best Practices is eager to provide support and technical assistance to all states and territories interested in pursuing hydrogen energy production through the H2Hubs program or alternative federal funding and financing mechanisms.

FULL CONTENT:
![National Governors Association](https://www.nga.org/wp-content/themes/nga/images/branding/logo.svg)
![National Governors Association](https://www.nga.org/wp-content/themes/nga/images/branding/logo.svg)

# Federal Funding and Financing for Hydrogen Energy Production and Use

This brief discusses the $7 billion funding announcement for the Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs program administered by the U.S. Department of Energy, the growing nationwide interest in hydrogen energy generation, and alternative federal funding and financing mechanisms for hydrogen generation projects.

([Download](https://www.nga.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Hydrogen_Hubs_Brief_Oct2023.pdf))

## **Hydrogen As an Energy Source**

Hydrogen is the most simple and abundant element in the universe. While highly combustible and energy-dense, hydrogen only naturally occurs on earth in compounds formed with other elements such as with oxygen to form water (H2O) and carbon to form hydrocarbons. These hydrocarbons are found in fossil fuels among other resources. Although chemical and industrial uses for hydrogen have been broadly deployed for more than a century, recent market and government investment in hydrogen as an energy source has broadened interest in hydrogen production.

Hydrogen energy is often categorized by producers, marketers, government agencies, and other stakeholders in color categories. Three of the most common color categories for hydrogen are green, pink, and blue:

It is important to note that, while some consensus around the definition of these color categories is forming, there is not a strict statutory or regulatory definition behind this system. Currently, the color system plays more of an informal marketing role.

In March 2023, NGA published a primer titled “[Hydrogen as an Energy Source](https://www.nga.org/publications/hydrogen-as-an-energy-source/)” that provides an overview of hydrogen energy generation, the various classifications of hydrogen energy, hydrogen energy use-cases, and federal support for hydrogen energy production.

## **H2Hubs: Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs Program**

#### **Program Overview**

In 2021, the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) established the [Hydrogen Earth Shot Challenge](https://www.energy.gov/eere/fuelcells/hydrogen-shot), aiming to reduce the cost of hydrogen as an energy source by 80% in a decade (reducing the cost per kilogram from the current $5 to $1). As part of this goal, the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act included $7 billion in funding for the Regional Clean Hydrogen Hubs ([H2Hubs](https://www.energy.gov/oced/regional-clean-hydrogen-hubs)) program. The H2Hubs program was created to fund between six to ten regional clean hydrogen hubs across the United States. Selected applicants would agree to a 1:1 cost share with DOE for construction and deployment of the hub.

The initial funding announcement was released in September 2022, and 79 applicants sent letters of interest to DOE. Of those, DOE selected 33 applicants to move forward with applications. In October 2023, DOE announced the selection of seven projects to receive hydrogen hubs grants.

#### **Funding Announcement**

On October 13, 2023, President Joe Biden and U.S. Secretary of Energy Jennifer Granholm [announced](https://www.energy.gov/oced/regional-clean-hydrogen-hubs-selections-award-negotiations) the seven projects selected for funding through the H2Hubs program:

**Appalachian Regional Clean Hydrogen Hub** ([ARCH2](https://www.arch2hub.com/))

**Alliance for Renewable Clean Hydrogen Energy Systems** ([ARCHES](https://archesh2.org/))

[**HyVelocity H2Hub**](https://www.hyvelocityhub.com/)

**Heartland Hydrogen Hub (HH2H)**

**Mid-Atlantic Clean Hydrogen Hub (**[**MACH2**](https://mach-2.com/)**)**

**Midwest Alliance for Clean Hydrogen (**[**MachH2**](https://machh2.com/)**)**

**Pacific Northwest Hydrogen Hub (**[**PNW H2**](https://pnwh2.com/)**)**

![](https://www.nga.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/hydrogen.png)

## **Alternative Federal Funding & Financing Mechanisms**

In addition to the H2Hubs program, there are many federal programs and financing mechanisms that can be utilized by states and territories for hydrogen energy projects. Some of these, such as those through the DOE Loan Programs Office, cannot be used to supplement federal grant funding, while other mechanisms may be combined.

#### [DOE Loan Programs Office (LPO)](https://www.energy.gov/lpo/articles/lpo-tech-talk-hydrogen)

The DOE LPO has approximately [$300 billion in loan authority](https://www.energy.gov/lpo/articles/program-guidance-title-17-clean-energy-program) to finance projects that support clean energy deployment and energy infrastructure reinvestment to reduce emissions, including:

**Innovative Energy**

**Innovative Supply Chain**

**State Energy Financing Institution (SEFI)**

**Energy Infrastructure Reinvestment (EIR)**

#### **DOE Grant Funding**

[Clean Hydrogen Electrolysis Program](https://www.energy.gov/eere/clean-hydrogen-electrolysis-program)

[Clean Hydrogen Manufacturing Recycling Program](https://www.energy.gov/eere/clean-hydrogen-manufacturing-recycling)

#### [**Tax credits and incentives**](https://www.energy.gov/eere/fuelcells/financial-incentives-hydrogen-and-fuel-cell-projects)

**45V: Clean Hydrogen Production Tax Credit**

**48C: Advanced Energy Project Credit**

**45Q: Carbon Capture and Sequestration Tax Credit**

## **NGA Support**

The NGA Center for Best Practices is eager to provide support and technical assistance to all states and territories interested in pursuing hydrogen energy production through the H2Hubs program or alternative federal funding and financing mechanisms. For more information on hydrogen energy generation, the H2Hubs program, or other federal programs, please contact Dan Lauf, Energy Program Director, at [dlauf@nga.org](mailto:dlauf@nga.org) or Richard Lukas, Legislative Director, at [rlukas@nga.org](mailto:rlukas@nga.org).

*This brief was prepared by Fiona Forrester, Policy Analyst, NGA Center for Best Practices.*

Funding Opportunities
=====================

Find information about open funding opportunity announcements (FOAs) and FOA project selections from the DOE Hydrogen Program's participating offices.

Funding Opportunities by Participating Offices
----------------------------------------------

### Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations

DOE's [Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations](https://www.energy.gov/oced/office-clean-energy-demonstrations) is a multi-technology office that includes the [regional hydrogen hubs](https://www.energy.gov/oced/regional-clean-hydrogen-hubs) program.

### Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE)

EERE's [Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office](https://www.energy.gov/eere/fuelcells/fuel-cell-technologies-office-funding-opportunities) issues solicitations for hydrogen and fuel cells technologies.

### Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management

DOE maintains a list of grant and contract opportunities for the [Office of Fossil Energy and Carbon Management](https://www.energy.gov/fe/solicitations-and-business-opportunities), through the National Energy Technology Laboratory procurement system.

### Office of Nuclear Energy

See DOE's [Office of Nuclear Energy](https://www.energy.gov/ne/) website for announcements about financial opportunities and solicitations.

### Office of Science

DOE's [Office of Science](https://www.energy.gov/science/office-science-funding/office-science-funding-opportunities) maintains a list of open and recent funding opportunities. Small businesses may be more interested in the [Small Business Innovations Research (SBIR)/Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) Programs](https://www.energy.gov/science/sbir/small-business-innovation-research-and-small-business-technology-transfer), also managed by DOE's Office of Science.

## Breadcrumb

# Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office Funding Opportunities

The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office (HFTO) in the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE) offers information about open and closed funding opportunities, requests for information, and links to related opportunities from other DOE offices and federal organizations.

The HFTO mission is to research, develop, and validate hydrogen production, delivery, storage, and fuel cell technologies. In carrying out this mission, HFTO selects research and development (R&D) and other projects through open and competitive procurements and encourages collaborative partnerships among industry; universities; national laboratories; federal, state, and local governments; and non-government agencies.

[Subscribe](/eere/fuelcells/subscribe-hydrogen-and-fuel-cell-news) to receive news and funding opportunity updates by email.

Find more information about the [application process](/eere/funding/how-do-i-apply-eere-funding).

Interested in becoming a project reviewer? HFTO is [looking for reviewers](/eere/fuelcells/interested-becoming-hydrogen-and-fuel-cell-project-reviewer "Interested in Becoming a Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Project Reviewer?") to review the merit of applications for federal funding opportunities and ongoing projects.

## Open Funding Opportunities

There are no current open HFTO funding opportunities.

## Requests for Information

There are no open HFTO requests for information at this time.

View [past requests for information](/eere/fuelcells/hydrogen-and-fuel-cell-technologies-office-past-requests-information).

## Closed Funding Opportunities

View [closed funding opportunities](/eere/fuelcells/hydrogen-and-fuel-cell-technologies-office-closed-funding-opportunities).

## Information for Award Recipients

View slides from the HFTO webinar, [Award Administration and Reporting Requirements for EERE Cooperative Agreements](/eere/fuelcells/articles/award-administration-and-reporting-requirements-eere-cooperative-agreements "Read more about Award Administration and Reporting Requirements for EERE Cooperative Agreements"), presented on December 5, 2017.

## Other Funding Opportunities

Find [funding opportunities outside of the Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technologies Office](/eere/fuelcells/hydrogen-and-fuel-cell-technologies-office-other-funding-opportunities).

--- SOURCE 4: $3.7 Million Is Available To Support Clean Hydrogen ... - nyserda ---
URL: https://www.nyserda.ny.gov/About/Newsroom/2025-Announcements/2025-06-09-NYSERDA-Announces-3-Million-Available-To-Support-Clean-Hydrogen-Resources

SUMMARY:
The New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA) today announced $3.7 million is now available to support clean hydrogen-based fuel cell resources in New York. The competitive solicitation, offered through NYSERDA’s Advanced Fuels and Thermal Energy Storage Program, seeks qualified proposers to develop workable designs for clean hydrogen-based fuel cell solutions to serve as firm capacity for a balanced electricity grid, or to help decarbonize industrial manufacturing processes. **National Association of State Energy Officials (NASEO) President David Terry said**, "NYSERDA's investment in hydrogen-based fuel cell technology development and demonstration projects is helping to drive innovation and economic development opportunities in New York while offering a tool to meet the critical electric reliability and resilience challenges that NYSERDA and other State Energy Offices across the country are working to address."

enriched_points.expanded_content: Recognizing diverse funding sources, including federal programs like the H2Hubs initiative and state-level grants, is essential for developing hydrogen infrastructure projects.
Balancing public funding, such as federal grants and state initiatives, with private investments encourages collaboration and ensures a robust financial foundation for hydrogen projects.
Implementing models like public-private partnerships and green bonds can attract diverse investors and foster innovation in hydrogen fuel infrastructure development.
Establishing cost-sharing frameworks among stakeholders can reduce financial burdens, enabling more projects to proceed while promoting shared ownership and commitment.
Developing strategies for long-term financial sustainability involves diversifying funding sources and creating revenue-generating mechanisms to support ongoing operations and maintenance.
